task.description: Repair unique_emails so it returns normalized unique emails in first-seen order. Accept each row as a string or as a dict with email, mail, or nested contact.email. Trim whitespace, lowercase emails, skip blanks, and skip invalid emails without '@'. Preserve first-seen canonical order after normalization.

Repair published_skus so it returns published product SKUs sorted by normalized SKU. Read the SKU from sku or nested product.sku, trim it, skip missing, blank, None, or malformed SKU sources, and collapse duplicate normalized SKUs. Trim and lowercase string status values before comparison. Published status tokens are 'published', 'live', '1', and True; missing, blank, and all other status values are unpublished. For duplicate SKUs, choose the status row by source priority first (catalog > feed > manual > missing/other), then by greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, then by later input order. Include only SKUs whose winning row is published.

task.description: Repair hours_by_employee so it returns total non-rejected hours by employee id. Accept employee ids from employee_id, employee, or nested employee.id, trimming employee ids before grouping. If a row_id is present, duplicate row_ids represent revisions: keep only the revision with the greatest numeric submitted_at timestamp, breaking timestamp ties by later input order, before applying status and totals. Ignore rows whose trimmed, lowercased status is rejected or void after revision selection. Parse hours from numbers and numeric strings; missing, blank, or None hours are 0. Preserve first-seen employee order based on accepted rows in the returned dict.

Repair normalize_accounts so it returns accounts sorted by id with the exact keys id, owner, email, and enabled. Accept owner from owner, name, or nested account.owner. Accept email from email, email_address, or nested contact.email. Trim and title-case owner, trim and lowercase email, and default missing owner/email to ''. Enabled tokens 'enabled', 'active', 'yes', 'true', '1', and True mean True; 'disabled', 'inactive', 'no', 'false', '0', False, missing, blank, None, and unknown tokens mean False. Merge duplicate ids by preferring enabled records for the enabled flag. For owner and email, use the nonblank value from the greatest numeric updated_at timestamp; rows without updated_at behave as timestamp 0, and ties are broken by later input order.

Repair subscription_flags so it returns a dict from normalized plan names to booleans. Read the plan from plan, name, or subscription.plan, trim and lowercase it, and skip blank plans. Trim and lowercase string flag values before comparison. Values 'enabled', 'yes', '1', and True mean True; every other value, missing value, blank string, and None means False. For duplicate plans, choose the winning row by source priority first (billing > crm > import > missing/other), then by greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, then by later input order. The winning row's normalized boolean is the output value.
